title: Human Relationships and Social Bonds
summary: How the brain's social homeostasis circuits, empathy systems, and hormones shape friendship, romance, attachment, and recovery from social loss.

context.headline: Social bonds are biological needs, not optional extras
context.description: The source presents social bonding as a homeostatic system, similar to hunger. We are pushed to seek connection through specific circuits and neurochemicals, then stabilized through shared physiology, empathy, and long-term attachment mechanisms. This framework helps explain friendship, love, loneliness, and breakup pain.
context.when: Across daily life: family, friendship, romance, and online networks
